description: Master registry for the primitives_v2 graph and per-thinker commitment portal scaffolding.

primitives.label: Substrate Structure
primitives.definition: The basic ontological architecture of what is self-standing and what is dependent.

primitives.label: Manifestation Status
primitives.definition: The status of the world, appearance, or finite objectivity relative to what grounds it.

primitives.label: Identity Relation
primitives.definition: The formal relation between ultimate reality, self, and world where unity and difference are both at issue.

primitives.label: Individuation Status
primitives.definition: The standing of the individual as individual.

primitives.label: Causation Model
primitives.definition: How the finite order issues from its ground, if it does.

primitives.label: Selfhood Structure
primitives.definition: What kind of self or subject is treated as real or operative.

primitives.label: Constitution Structure
primitives.definition: How subject-object articulation is constituted, or refused, at the level of experience itself.

primitives.label: Finite Cognition Model
primitives.definition: The account of error, finitude, obscuration, or interpretation.

primitives.label: Epistemic Authority
primitives.definition: The dominant source or ordered set of sources by which load-bearing claims are warranted.

primitives.label: Determination Operator
primitives.definition: The main operator by which determinacy or difference is articulated.

primitives.label: Method of Critique
primitives.definition: The operative procedure by which claims are advanced, tested, or dismantled.

primitives.label: Semantic Mediation
primitives.definition: How language or sign mediates access to reality, action, or objecthood.

primitives.label: Temporal Mode
primitives.definition: Whether being is framed as substance, process, disclosure, or a layered combination.

primitives.label: Register of Evolution
primitives.definition: How emergence, ascent, or historic transformation is treated.

primitives.label: Modal Structure of Truth
primitives.definition: How truth is distributed when multiple standpoints or levels are in play.

primitives.label: Relation to Perspectivism
primitives.definition: How plurality of viewpoints or standpoints is treated.

primitives.label: Standpoint Predication
primitives.definition: How predication itself is qualified, ranked, or multiplied across standpoints.

primitives.label: Normative Order Source
primitives.definition: Where obligation or binding normativity is sourced.

primitives.label: Social Formation Model
primitives.definition: How institutions, social form, or collective structures generate subjects and objectivity.

primitives.label: Affective Motive Force
primitives.definition: What fundamentally moves life, practice, or transformation.

primitives.label: Practice Path
primitives.definition: The dominant practical path by which transformation or release is pursued.

primitives.label: Soteric End
primitives.definition: The final end aimed at or described by the thinker.

commitment_thinker_to_primitive.citation_locus: History of Sexuality, vol. 1, Part II
The Matter with Things, vol. II, ch. 27
The Master and His Emissary, Part Two
The Matter with Things, vol. II, chs. 20-28

cross_engagement.brief: Karl Marx works the same finite cognition axis in the political-social register. Here the operative value is `genealogically-produced-illusion`; Karl Marx lands on that same value.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `dialectical-development`.
Judith Butler presses the same normativity axis in the political-social register, but with the value `disciplinary-power` rather than `class-structured-social-relation`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same social formation axis in the political-social register, but with the value `recognitive-institutional` rather than `commodity-fetish-social-form`.
Abhinavagupta works the same motive force axis in the aesthetic register. Here the operative value is `aesthetic-rapture`; Abhinavagupta lands on that same value.

Adi Sankara touches the same soteric end axis, but not in the same register. This section works it as `ethical-vigilance-without-final-fusion` in the ethical register; Adi Sankara approaches it through the soteriological register.
